title: foeniculum vulgare subsp. sativum
intro: Foeniculum vulgare subsp. sativum, also known as garden or common wild fennel, is a subspecies of the herbaceous perennial plant Foeniculum vulgare. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for centuries for its medicinal and culinary properties. The medicinal properties of Foeniculum vulgare subsp. sativum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and carminative effects, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, flatulence, and menstrual cramps. The plant's health benefits include reducing anxiety and improving respiratory health, and it can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as tea, essential oil, and dried seeds.
